Re: Robert Bascherini v. University of San Francisco, et al.
San Francisco Superior Court, Case No. CGC-18-567239
Dear Mr. Fletcher:
This office represents the University of San Francisco (“the University”). Please direct all
further communications on this matter to our office.
Enclosed is your client’s employment agreement with the University dated December 19,
2012.
This is to inform you that within that agreement, there is a binding arbitration provision.
Please dismiss the complaint and enter into arbitration to adjudicate the claims of the
complaint.
If you do not dismiss the complaint and enter into arbitration pursuant to the offer letter, I
will forthwith file with the Court, a petition to compel arbitration.
I invite you to call me to discuss. Thank you.
Very truly yours,
VARTAIN LAW GROUP
Michael J. Vartain

Dear Mr. Azcarate-Bascheriai:
The University of San Francisco is pleased to offer you the position of Iniramura/Club Sports Dirsetor
within Recreational Sports/Koret, commencing on January 2, 2013. This letter specifies the terms of the
offer. Intramural/Club Sports Director is an exempt position, meaning it is a salary (and not hourly)
paid position. Your yemi-menthiy salary will be $2,556.25 aubject to all applicable withholdings and
deductions.
Should you accept this offer, your duties will be ag described on the attached job description. You will
report to Charles White, Senior Director.
The Administrative Staff Handbook describes other terms and conditions of employment with the
University and can be referenced at http://www .usfta. edu/HR/Employment/Table_of Contenta/.
You art eligible for University provided benefits, subject to the terms of policies and benefits plans,
which may change from time to time, To schedule your Benefits Orientation please register online at
alumrlustca edu/benafiigorieniation. Because your start date is January 2, 2015, the earliest your
medical coverage will begin fs February 1, 2014. Delay in scheduling your benefits orientation could
delay the date of ymir medical coverage.
Your employment at the Univecalty will be "at will" as defined by California law. The University may
conclude your employment for any reason or forno reason, with or without notice, Likewise, you may
conclude your employment at any time for any reason or for no reason, with or without notice, No
person other than a Vice-President of the University may change this at-will enployment relationship
and auch change must be made in writing and signed by the Vice-President,
Any and all [eal disputes initiated by either you or the University must be subraitted only to binding
ndtitcal arbitration, befors a mutually chosen arbitrator, and not to's court, judge orjury. Both parties —
have the right to be represented by counsel of their choice; each is entitled to discovery consistent with
law; and to obtain those remedies for claims and defenses ag would be ordered by a court. The
University will pay’ all of the professional fees of the arbitrator unless you object. The arbitrator will...
issue a written award that must be accompanied by a written staterneat of decision. Lega! disputes
“Ynelude those araing under this contract or under federal, state and/or local statutes; the commoniaw of
California} all contract ead tort. and other legal matters; bul excluding unemployment benefits, workers’ -. — >
compensation banefits, the Labor Management Relations Act of 1947, and EEOC, DFEH, and/or Stats _

 
Labor Commissionee wage complaints, which complaints may be filed with the government agencies
This letter contains the entire agreement between you and the University on the terms of your
employment and supersedes any and sll prior or contemporaneous agreements or understandings. The
terms of your employment as stated herein will be governed by and construed under the laws of the State
of California, and may not be modified unless in writing signed by you and the University.
Please confirm your acceptance of this offer by signing, dating, and returning this letter to the Human
Resources Office, atin: Jennifer Heap. .
; We look forward to working with you in your new position.

Sincerely yours, , .
Diane L Nelson

Job Title: Intramural/Club Sports Director
Department: Recreational Sports/Koret ‘
Position Number: G$9813
Job Description
Reporting directly to the Sr. Director of the Recreational Sports Department, the Director is responsible
to oversee all aspects of Intramural Sports, Club Sports, and Equipment Desk, and Locker Roam
operations. Responsibilities include compliance and implementations of comprehensive, safety
standards, budget aversight, custamer service, business operations, facility upkeep, and all related.
programs. This position requires excellence and effective leadership skills and outstanding supervisory
abilities to providing guidance to all relevant staff, members and perticipants.
Asa member of the larger staff in Student Life, the Director of the Recreational Sports Department's
Intramural/Club Sports/Equipment Desk operations collaborates and cooperates to create leadership and ;
intercultural competence opportnnities to support student leaming and development in the Jesuit
Catholic tradition. The mission of Student Life is to fully support holistic studeat development within a
; social justice framework centered in preparing students to be caring, socially responsible citizens in our
global and local community.

* Hire, supervise and evaluate Administrative Staff, Grad Assistants, Coaches, Professional .
Officials, and sll student staff as relevant to this position

eavironment for all participants and that all staff are proactively trained and evaluated and all paper

maintaining strong enrollments and exceptional user and participant satisfaction.
* Set policy and operating standards for all event and functions including: safety, customer
service, staff accountability, programming, member services, budgetary accountability, etc. Ensure
these objective and accountabilities are consistently met. ‘
* Provide clear, consistent and effective direction and communication fo all staff, participants, and
members
Manage and oversee all business operations and systems including: administrative staff,
registration, payroll, record maintenance and stafYparticipant communication.

serv

Program and marketing strategy for all areas of responsibility

* Ability to perform other duties as needed, such as coaching, teaching, instruction,

* Be committed to the mission and goals of USF. Participate in and promote USF eventa and
programs outside of own department. . ;
